reasoning: Query asks for cherry blossom (sakura) spots in Tohoku, not Tokyo. Tool fetched Akita spots, which is one Tohoku prefecture and does include Kakunodate (sakura_meisho_100, cherry blossom famous spot) in the truncated data. However, the response surfaces Lake Tazawa, Mount Chokai, a fireworks festival — mostly non-sakura spots. The single clear cherry blossom result (Kakunodate) appears late. Coverage is Akita-only, not multi-prefecture Tohoku as requested. Constraint on Tohoku breadth largely ignored.
missing_data: Miyagi (Hitome Senbonzakura), Iwate (Koiwai Farm), Yamagata (Kajo Park), Aomori (Hirosaki Castle) — all iconic Tohoku sakura spots absent. No seasonal metadata.
improvement_hint: Filter or rank by sakura_meisho_100 kind when cherry blossom intent is detected. Expand coverage to all Tohoku prefectures (Aomori, Iwate, Miyagi, Yamagata, Fukushima) rather than single prefecture.

reasoning: Query asks for major Niigata sake breweries. Response returns 24 items via get_local_specialty, surfacing 越乃寒梅, 八海山, 久保田 — all top Niigata brands with QIDs, descriptions, authority attribution. Mix includes GI food items (edamame) which dilutes focus, but core sake brands are well covered. Factually accurate and grounded in METI/Wikidata sources.
missing_data: Brewery visit addresses and opening hours are absent. Some sake brands (e.g. 〆張鶴, 菊水) not visible in truncated output but may be present. Brewery tour experience info missing.
improvement_hint: Add q-filter keyword support so callers can narrow to sake_brand/sake_brewery kinds rather than returning all food GIs. Brewery physical address data would aid trip planning.

reasoning: Query asks for ski resorts with hot springs (onsen) in Hakuba. Response returns Hakuba Happo-One (ski resort, ✓) and Hakuba Oike (lake), a UN tourism award notice, and an art museum — none of the latter are ski+onsen combos. The onsen constraint is not addressed. Hakuba does have onsen-equipped lodges (Tsugaike Kogen, Happo-One village onsens) but these do not appear.
missing_data: Onsen facilities near Hakuba ski resorts: Hakuba Happo no Yu, Mimizuku no Yu, accommodations with in-house onsen. No onsen-typed spots visible in response.
improvement_hint: When query combines ski+onsen, consider also calling get_hotels filtered for onsen_ryokan in the same municipality. Add onsen kind-tags to relevant ski resort spots in the corpus.

reasoning: Query asks for autumn foliage (koyo) in Kansai region. Tool fetches Kyoto spots, which is reasonable as Kyoto is prime koyo territory, but response surfaces Nijo Castle, To-ji Temple, and Nagaoka-kyo — none are specifically tagged for autumn foliage. No koyo or momiji kind-tag filtering. Sanzen-in (famous koyo) appears late. Kansai as multi-prefecture region (Nara, Osaka, Hyogo) completely absent.
missing_data: Nara, Osaka, Hyogo koyo spots. Kyoto-specific koyo spots: Tofuku-ji, Eikan-do, Arashiyama, Kibune. Seasonal date metadata.
improvement_hint: Add koyo/momiji kind tag to known autumn foliage spots. When intent is koyo+Kansai, query multiple prefectures or use search_semantic with 'autumn foliage Kansai' to retrieve relevant spots across the region.

reasoning: Arabic query asks for famous cherry blossom spots in Kansai. Tool fetches Nara spots — good choice as Yoshino (top result) is Japan's most iconic sakura site with sakura_meisho_100 tag. Also includes Koriyama Castle (known for moat sakura). However Takamatsuzuka Tomb (kofun, not sakura-related) and Kofuku-ji surface without sakura relevance. Kansai breadth (Osaka, Kyoto, Hyogo) absent.
missing_data: Osaka (Expo/Osaka Castle), Kyoto (Maruyama Park, Arashiyama), Hyogo sakura spots. More sakura-specific spots within Nara itself (Yoshimizu Shrine, Narazaka).
improvement_hint: Filter/boost sakura_meisho_100 tagged spots when cherry blossom intent detected. Expand to multi-prefecture when regional (Kansai) query is made rather than single prefecture.

reasoning: Query asks for stargazing spots on Okinawa's remote islands. Tool fetches Okinawa spots broadly. Response leads with Nakagusuku Castle, Tamaudun, Shikina-en — all Okinawa main island UNESCO-adjacent heritage sites, not remote island stargazing. Taketomi Island appears in truncated area but no stargazing kind-tag exists. No astrotourism or remote island filtering possible with current data.
missing_data: Iriomote, Yonaguni, Miyakojima, Ishigaki Island stargazing spots. No night sky/astrotourism metadata in corpus. Remote island (離島) filtering absent.
improvement_hint: Add astro_tourism or night_sky kind tag to known stargazing locations. Tag Yaeyama/Miyako island cluster spots. Consider get_spots municipality filter for specific remote islands rather than prefecture-level only.

reasoning: Thai query asks for rural onsen accommodation in Hokkaido. Response returns get_hotels with multiple 日本秘湯を守る会 (secret hot spring) ryokan — Shiranai Onsen, Ginkonyu, Tokachidake, Yukomanbetsu — all genuinely rural Hokkaido onsen. Excellent intent match. However coordinates are null for all hito_yu_kai entries limiting practical usefulness. ANA Holiday Inn Sapporo (city hotel) appears in truncated list — not rural.
missing_data: Coordinates for onsen ryokan would greatly help. Municipality labels for rural ryokans are null. Description not translated to Thai.
improvement_hint: Enrich hito_yu_kai entries with geocoded coordinates from source pages. Filter out urban hotels (type=hotel, city coordinates) when rural+onsen constraint is explicit.

reasoning: Vietnamese query asks for Seto Inland Sea islands. Tool fetches Kagawa spots. Response leads with Ritsurin Garden and Manno Lake — beautiful but mainland Kagawa, not islands. Marugame Castle, Takamatsu Castle also mainland. The Seto Inland Sea island content (Naoshima, Teshima, Shodoshima) may be partially in corpus but does not surface in visible truncated response. Major constraint failure on island focus.
missing_data: Naoshima (art island), Teshima, Shodoshima, Ogijima, Megijima, Oshima. Hiroshima-side Itsukushima. Multi-prefecture Seto scope (Hiroshima, Okayama) absent.
improvement_hint: Add island kind-tag to Seto Inland Sea islands. When 'islands' intent is detected, filter by kind=island or search_semantic. Include Hiroshima (Miyajima) and Okayama in Seto queries.

reasoning: Query asks for indigo dyeing workshops in Tokushima. Response returns 3 METI craft items: Awa Hon-ai Shijira Weave (indigo textile ✓), Otani Ware (ceramics, not indigo), Awa Washi (indigo-dyed paper ✓). The core indigo craft is present and well-described. However only 3 items, and the query specifically asks for 'workshops' (体験・工房) — addresses and visit logistics are absent. Description for Shijira Weave is null.
missing_data: Workshop venues with addresses (e.g. Awa Ai Kanko Center). Indigo dyeing experience (藍染体験) venues. Craft descriptions for meti_densan:0122 are null.
improvement_hint: Scrape craft workshop/experience venues from Tokushima tourism DMO site. Add description data for meti_densan:0122 (Shijira Weave). Consider adding experience_venue kind tag for workshop locations.

reasoning: German query asks for Buddhist temple lodging (shukubo) on Mount Koya. get_hotels returns multiple 高野山宿坊協会 confirmed shukubo entries with phone numbers, descriptions, and source URLs — excellent intent match. Annyoin, Ichijoin, Ekoin, Kitamuroin all appear with rich Japanese description of each temple's character. This is best-in-class for this query type. Minor gap: no English descriptions for shukubo, coordinates null.
missing_data: English translations of shukubo descriptions. Coordinates for individual temples. Website URLs for most entries. Price range information.
improvement_hint: Add English translations for koyasan_shukubo description_ja fields. Geocode shukubo entries from the association's website. Add website URLs from source pages.

reasoning: Query asks for samurai districts outside Kyoto. search_area returns 146 results for 武家屋敷. Top results include Matsue (Shimane), Fukui (Uchiyama), Kakunodate (Akita), Chiran (Kagoshima) — all geographically diverse and authentic samurai districts far from Kyoto. Kakunodate and Chiran are genuine highlights with heritage designations. No Kyoto entry visible in top 5. High-quality response.
missing_data: Kitsuki (Oita), Kiso-Fukushima (Nagano), Izushi (Hyogo) samurai districts. Heritage designation context for some entries is null.
improvement_hint: Surface prominence_score more effectively for buke_yashiki results. Add jokamachi and preservation_district compound filter to improve ranking of authentic districts over generic museum entries.

reasoning: Indonesian query asks for summer festivals in Akita. get_festivals returns 3 items: Dainichido Bugaku (New Year sacred dance, not summer), Kakunodate Festival (autumn), Tsuchizaki Shrine Festival. Major summer festivals Kanto Matsuri and Omagari Fireworks — both iconic Akita summer events — are absent from items. UNESCO section includes Hayachine Kagura (Iwate, not Akita). Critical coverage failure for the summer constraint.
missing_data: 秋田竿燈まつり (Kanto Festival, Aug) and 大曲の花火 (Omagari Fireworks, Aug) are iconic Akita summer festivals but absent from festival items. A Wikidata spot for Omagari was in L2-06 but not surfaced here.
wrong_data: Hayachine Kagura in national_heritage section is actually an Iwate Prefecture item, shown in Akita context.
improvement_hint: Improve festival scraping to capture Kanto Matsuri and Omagari from Akita tourism portal. Add seasonal date filter support so summer festivals (June-August) can be ranked first.

reasoning: Korean query asks for small fishing harbor towns in Sanin region. Tool fetches Shimane spots. Response surfaces Lake Shinji, Matsue Castle, Iwami Ginzan Silver Mine, Mount Sanbe — all notable but none are fishing port towns (漁港 or 漁村). Shimane does have port towns like Hamada and Mihonoseki but they don't appear. Tottori-side (Sakaiminato, Uradome) also absent.
missing_data: Mihonoseki (mythological port town), Hamada port, Sakaiminato (Tottori), Uradome Coast fishing villages. No port_town or fishing_village kind tags in corpus.
improvement_hint: Add port_town, fishing_village, gyoko kind tags to relevant coastal municipalities. Include Tottori in Sanin-region queries. Consider a search_semantic query for '漁港 山陰' as supplement to get_spots.

reasoning: Query asks where to experience washi paper making in Gifu. Response returns 8 craft items. Mino Ware (ceramics), Hida Shunkei (lacquer), Nagiso Rokuro (woodwork), Ichii Ittobori appear before washi items. The query specifically asks about washi experience locations — no venue addresses or workshop details available. Mino Washi (美濃和紙) should appear but is not visible in truncated output; the response may contain it further down.
missing_data: 美濃和紙 (Mino Washi, meti_densan:0904) workshop venues with addresses. Udatsu no Machinami washi experience center. Itadori washi experience information.
improvement_hint: Add keyword search support within get_local_specialty so 'washi' filters to paper crafts. Add experience_venue data for craft workshop locations. Consider ranking washi items first when washi is in query.

reasoning: Spanish query asks for fishing villages on Noto Peninsula. Tool fetches Ishikawa prefecture broadly. Response surfaces Kanazawa Castle, Kenroku-en, Bed and Craft hotel, Nanao Castle — none are fishing villages on Noto. Noto Peninsula fishing villages like Wajima (漆), Sosogi, Noto Ogi, Suzu are absent. The peninsula-specific constraint is completely missed.
missing_data: Wajima (輪島), Sosogi coast, Suzu, Anamizu fishing ports on Noto Peninsula. No municipality filter was applied for Noto-specific towns. Noto earthquake (2024) context relevant for current access.
improvement_hint: Support municipality-level query for Noto-area towns (Wajima, Suzu, Noto-cho). Add fishing_port, gyoson kind tags. The 2024 Noto earthquake may have affected site access — consider adding status flags.

reasoning: Query asks for lesser-known sand dunes beyond Tottori. search_area returns 90 results. Top hits include Tottori (expected, appears first), then Onjuku Beach (Chiba), Ishikari sand dune museum (Hokkaido), Fukiagehama (Kagoshima), Tsujido Beach (Kanagawa). Diverse national coverage satisfies 'not just Tottori' constraint well. Good geographic spread. Film location tourism entry for Tottori is minor noise.
missing_data: Sakata Minamiura dunes (Yamaguchi), Nakatajima sand dunes (Hamamatsu) are absent. Some entries lack description_en.
improvement_hint: Add description_en to sand_dune entries missing it. Deprioritize nav/promotional text municipal_scrape entries that lack spot identity (e.g. Tottori film commission page is noise here).

reasoning: Chinese query asks for traditional crafts in Sanin region. Tool queries Shimane (one Sanin prefecture) with Chinese lang. Returns 4 METI designated crafts: Iwami Ware (ceramics), Sekishu Washi (paper, UNESCO-linked), Unshu Soroban (abacus), Izumo Stone Lantern — all genuinely Shimane/Sanin crafts with high-quality Chinese translations and official descriptions. Tottori-side crafts absent but Shimane alone is rich.
missing_data: Tottori crafts (因州和紙, 弓浜絣) absent as only Shimane was queried. see_also_wikidata_venues shows Iwami Ginzan — relevant context.
improvement_hint: For regional queries (山陰 = Shimane + Tottori), automatically query both prefectures and merge results. Consider adding region parameter to get_local_specialty.

reasoning: Query asks for lavender fields in Hokkaido beyond Furano. Tool fetches Hokkaido spots broadly. Response surfaces Showa-Shinzan volcano, Sapporo Clock Tower, Matsumae Castle, Daisetsuzan — none relate to lavender. No lavender-kind tag exists. Furano should be present but is not visible. Lesser-known lavender fields (Biei, Kamifurano) are completely absent. Major retrieval failure for a seasonal flower tourism query.
missing_data: Furano lavender fields, Biei Shikisai-no-oka, Kamifurano lavender farms. No lavender or flower_field kind tag in corpus. Seasonal flower tourism entirely missing from data.
improvement_hint: Add lavender, flower_field, shikisai kind tags. Scrape Hokkaido flower tourism pages from official prefecture portal. Consider semantic search fallback when kind-tag retrieval fails for seasonal queries.

reasoning: Filipino query asks for traditional old towns (古い町並み) in Kyushu. Tool fetches Oita spots. Response leads with Usuki Castle, Usa Jingu, Oka Castle — historically notable but not specifically 'traditional townscapes.' Usuki does have a preserved street area. The query maps to machiya/preservation districts in Kyushu: Yanagawa, Hita, Arita — none visible. Oita-only scope misses Kyushu breadth.
missing_data: Hita Mameda-machi (Oita, actually), Yanagawa (Fukuoka), Arita porcelain town (Saga), Yufuin historic buildings. Preservation districts across Kyushu.
improvement_hint: For Kyushu-region queries, query multiple prefectures. Filter preservation_district, jokamachi, machiya kind tags for 'old townscape' intent. Hita Mameda-machi is within Oita and should appear.

reasoning: Query asks for kominka (traditional farmhouse) stays in Hokuriku. Tool fetches Ishikawa hotels. Response shows 日本秘湯を守る会 onsen ryokan (not kominka), then 'Student housing 1' and 'AB Hotel' (completely irrelevant). Bed and Craft kominka network appeared in the spots response (L2-20) but not in hotels. Toyama and Fukui kominka absent. Kominka-specific lodging poorly served.
missing_data: Kominka stay properties: Bed and Craft (Inami, Toyama), traditional farmhouses in Shirakawa-go/Gokayama area (Toyama). Fukui kominka stays. kominka_stay type entries sparse.
wrong_data: 'Student housing 1' is noise data from OSM that should not surface for traveler queries.
improvement_hint: Filter out non-traveler lodging types (student housing, dormitory) from hotel results. Add Toyama and Fukui to Hokuriku kominka query. Enrich kominka_stay typed entries with descriptions and photos.

reasoning: Query asks for mountain shrines on Kii Peninsula. get_japan_heritage returns rich Wakayama heritage: Kongobu-ji (Koya-san head temple), Negoro-ji, Kumano Kodo pilgrimage route, Kumano Sanzan — all directly relevant mountain-worship sites. The tool_scope_note helpfully points to see_also for UNESCO WHS. Excellent coverage of Kii mountain faith landscape. QIDs, coordinates, heritage designations all present.
missing_data: Yoshino Mikumari Shrine, Nachi Falls (Kumano Nachi Taisha specifically), Omine-san (mountain ascetic practice) not visible in truncated text but likely present in full response.
improvement_hint: Surface the tool_scope_note content as structured field rather than free-text warning. Add see_also links to Mie-side Kumano Sanzan entries for complete Kii coverage.

reasoning: Query asks for summer fireworks festivals along Sea of Japan coast. get_festivals for Niigata returns 129 items. Third item is 長岡大花火大会 (Nagaoka Grand Fireworks) — iconic Sea of Japan fireworks event, correctly included. However first two items are spring flower festivals unrelated to fireworks or summer. The fireworks item surfaces only at position 3. Count of 129 with no seasonal pre-filtering makes agent work to find the relevant fireworks. Sea of Japan coast constraint not addressed (Akita, Ishikawa, Toyama fireworks absent).
missing_data: Niigata Nagaoka is good. But Akita Omagari, Ishikawa fireworks festivals, coastal scope beyond Niigata needed. Items not pre-filtered by season or type.
improvement_hint: Add event_type or month filter to get_festivals. When fireworks (花火) intent detected, prioritize hanabi-tagged spots. Extend coverage to coastal prefectures when 'Sea of Japan' scope requested.

reasoning: Query asks for traditional bamboo crafts in Oita. get_local_specialty returns 2 items: Hakata Weave (shared with Fukuoka/Saga, has Oita in prefecture_codes) and Beppu Bamboo Crafts — the iconic bamboo craft of Oita with excellent description, production area (Beppu, Oita, Hita, etc.), and METI designation. Beppu Bamboo is directly on-target. see_also_wikidata_venues adds museum context. Count=2 is sparse but the key item is present with high quality.
missing_data: Workshop visit information for Beppu bamboo craft centers. Hakata Ori is primarily Fukuoka — its presence may confuse though it is technically valid. Venue coordinates for craft centers.
improvement_hint: Add craft experience venue data for Beppu Bamboo Craft Center. The production_area_text should link to visitable workshops. Count=2 could be expanded with DMO-sourced bamboo craft workshop listings.

reasoning: Query asks for cycling routes through rural Shikoku. Tool fetches Ehime spots. Response leads with Ozu Castle, Yuzuki Castle, then Omishima Island (has cycling_route kind-tag ✓). The Shimanami Kaido (world-famous Shikoku cycling route) should be present but is not visible in top results. Kochi, Tokushima, Kagawa cycling content absent. The cycling constraint is weakly addressed.
missing_data: Shimanami Kaido (しまなみ海道) as primary cycling route. Kochi cycling routes. Shikoku Karst cycling. cycling_route kind-tagged spots beyond Omishima.
improvement_hint: When cycling_route intent is detected, filter/rank by kind=cycling_route tag. The Shimanami Kaido is a marquee asset — ensure it has cycling_route kind and high prominence. Multi-prefecture Shikoku queries should span all 4 prefectures.

reasoning: Query asks where to see kabuki outside Tokyo/Kyoto. get_traditional_arts returns 183 items starting with Bunraku, then Kabuki (Q199701) — correctly identifying the art form. However the response provides no venue locations. Kabuki is listed as UNESCO ICH but no specific regional kabuki theaters (Konpira Grand Theater in Kagawa, Shochikuza in Osaka, etc.) are linked. The data knows kabuki exists but cannot answer 'where' to see it regionally.
missing_data: Regional kabuki venues: Kanamaru-za (Kotohira/Kagawa, oldest surviving kabuki theater), Mibu Kyogen (Kyoto but outside main theaters), folk kabuki in rural areas. No venue-to-art linkage in corpus.
improvement_hint: Link traditional arts records to physical venue spots via get_spots. Add venue_for_art kind-tag. Kanamaru-za in Kagawa should be tagged as kabuki_venue. Consider adding a venue field to traditional arts records.
